title: lilac vulgaris var. coerulea
intro: Lilac vulgaris var. coerulea, commonly known as the blue lilac, is a variety of lilac shrub that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The blue lilac has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include reducing stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of lilac vulgaris var. coerulea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers or leaves in hot water or a solvent.
